flags.f2_scaffold_directive: ROOT-ADJUDICATED LOWER-BOUND INTERFACE — preserve every other accepted F2.5 correction and existing proof body. The first citation review accepted the documented control-zero specialization except for the unintended d=0 case; its requested repair was positive alphabet size, not replacement by a global ATE premise. Restore lem:zeng-one-arm-minimax-lower / ZengOneArmMinimaxLower to the cited fixed-sample control-zero one-arm interface over ControlZeroLaw and oneArmMinimaxRisk. Quantify n,d and require 0 < d before asserting a_epsilon * minimaxRate n d <= oneArmMinimaxRisk n d epsilon; retain strict 0<epsilon<1/2, positive a_epsilon,b_epsilon, cutoff N_epsilon, and d <= b_epsilon*n*log n. Restore lem:ate-lower-bound-transfer as the paper-owned restriction theorem: for positive d, derive the global minimaxRisk lower bound from hZeng by proving tau=treatedFunctional on every ControlZeroLaw and comparing the subclass supremum/infimum; hZeng must NOT already assume worstCaseMSE or minimaxRisk over the unrestricted ATE class. Propagate the intrinsic 0<d domain to headline consumers where needed. Update plan/graph descriptions to this source interface. Preserve the D0.5-attested source map (Theorem 2 + Appendix C.5 + Lemma 4/Appendix D.2), do not demand a verbatim standalone source theorem, do not reconstruct the cited proof, do not weaken any conclusion, and do not churn unrelated declarations. BOOKKEEPING: emit exactly one canonical -- @node: tag for each delivered obj_id, immediately before its primary declaration; never repeat an @node tag inside a declaration body or attach the same obj_id to a helper lemma.

proposed_from.topic: Sharp minimax ATE estimation with unrestricted high-dimensional discrete confounders: for iid (X,A,Y) with X in [d], binary A and Y, overlap epsilon <= P(A=1|X=k) <= 1-epsilon, and unrestricted cell probabilities and conditional response means, prove that the exact minimax MSE is asymptotically equivalent up to epsilon-dependent constants to 1/n + [d/(n log n)]^2 uniformly for d <= c n log n; construct a computable hybrid estimator using plug-in estimation on well-populated cells and polynomial/factorial-moment estimation on sparsely populated cells, pair it with the published moment-matching lower bound, and establish parametric MSE through d of order sqrt(n) log n and consistency through d=o(n log n), thereby closing the log-squared gap left by Zeng-Balakrishnan-Han-Kennedy (arXiv:2405.00118). Delegate the exact heavy/light split, polynomial basis and degree constants, Poissonization details, and variance normalization to derivation, but require a proved light-cell approximation-and-variance lemma and a verified two-category confounding witness.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: The closest published result is ZengBalakrishnanHanKennedy2024, which proves the lower bound n^{-1}+d^2/(n^2 log^2 n) but analyzes standard ATE estimators only at n^{-1}+d^2/n^2 and explicitly leaves polynomial estimators open. JiaoVenkatHanWeissman2015 and HanJiaoWeissman2020 provide hybrid estimators for noncausal discrete functionals, not a uniform approximation-and-variance result for the homogeneous overlap-restricted four-cell ATE functional. The proposed light-cell lemma is therefore the precise missing result rather than a relabeling of their methods.

seed_details.kernel: Match the published discrete-ATE moment-matching lower bound with a heavy-cell plug-in and light-cell factorial-moment polynomial estimator.
seed_details.rebuttal: The ATE cell contribution is a coupled overlap-restricted four-cell rational functional under arbitrary covariate masses, not a separable functional.

seed_details.kernel: Construct honest non-root-n inference for the sparse-cell polynomial ATE estimator.
seed_details.red_team: A standard studentized CLT should suffice.
seed_details.rebuttal: The polynomial approximation bias and heterogeneous factorial-moment variance are first-order in the sparse regime.

seed_details.kernel: Derive the joint minimax frontier in alphabet size, sample size, and deteriorating overlap.
seed_details.red_team: It is only fixed-overlap theory with constants exposed.
seed_details.rebuttal: Treatment-arm rarity and covariate-cell rarity interact inside factorial-moment variances and the lower-bound prior.

seed_details.kernel: Characterize the attainable efficiency bound as the discrete alphabet grows before sparse-cell irregularity dominates.
seed_details.red_team: The fixed-dimensional ATE efficient influence function already answers this.
seed_details.rebuttal: It does not characterize uniform attainment when the saturated nuisance dimension grows with n.

seed_details.kernel: Obtain minimax welfare regret for category-wise treatment policies using polynomial estimation of rare-cell treatment gains.
seed_details.red_team: ATE risk does not determine policy regret.
seed_details.rebuttal: Correct; the sign-loss geometry is distinct and would require its own margin-free lower bound.

seed_details.kernel: Invert a permutation test for the high-dimensional observational ATE.
seed_details.red_team: Unknown observational propensities destroy the treatment-label symmetry required for exact permutation validity.
seed_details.rebuttal: Objection holds without a new design or conditional randomization assumption.

seed_details.kernel: Sharpen the variance-weighted ATE rate when the covariate law is known.
seed_details.red_team: The anchor already supplies a second-order estimator for this structured target.
seed_details.rebuttal: Objection holds; it does not furnish a distinct unresolved kernel.

seed_details.kernel: Adapt the polynomial heavy/light estimator to an unknown overlap constant.
seed_details.red_team: This is a tuning detail rather than a theorem.
seed_details.rebuttal: Uniform adaptation changes the approximation domain and coefficient-variance control; retained as the core's open-ended follow-on.

proposed_from.literature_map: The M11(a) lane survives: Zeng et al. prove the lower rate and explicitly leave polynomial ATE estimators for future work; Jiao et al., Wu and Yang, and Han et al. provide the sparse/dense, factorial-moment, and rational-functional technique ancestors but do not analyze the causal four-cell functional. The M11(c) lane survives as a separate follow-on: Zeng et al. give fixed-d inference only, while McClean et al. require smooth regression structure. The M11(b) lane remains plausible but less sharp because fixed-d influence-function theory does not by itself determine a growing-alphabet efficiency frontier. The M16 policy seed was not chosen because its sign-loss and margin geometry is not implied by ATE risk. The M17 lane was eliminated after checking the observational anchor: unknown propensities invalidate the exchangeability needed for exact permutation inference.

banked_reason: Clean F5 with dual F4 convergence, zero proof holes or gates, and a field-tier novelty pass; user approved CKPT 2. The ZengOneArmMinimaxLower lower bound is cited and transferred to ATE, not rederived.
